data.narrative:
AnySignal is a Series A space and aerospace company that has raised $34M+ to date. The valuation analysis leverages a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Aalyria, ClearSpace, Hubble Network, and Sift) alongside public comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector, which includes five publicly traded names with a median P/S multiple of 53.2x.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $34M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, implying a $68M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four private comps yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples was not applicable due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, common among private companies. The peer valuation range of $274M–$1.3B reflects heterogeneity in stage and capital intensity across the comparable set.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $58M to $460M, with a median of $234M. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty in stage-appropriate comparability: stage-progression assumes minimal pricing discipline, while peer-median incorporates market pricing but spans both Series A and Series B rounds. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue run rate (necessary for public comp application) and which peer stage best reflects AnySignal's development trajectory and capital efficiency.
